user_query:
Please read the project spec in input/spec.md, the sample payloads in input/sample_payloads.json, and the implementation constraints in input/constraints.txt. Build a production-ready FastAPI service skeleton that implements the described endpoints and adheres to the constraints and best practices. Create these files:
- output/main.py (app with routers, lifespan-managed resources, and endpoints matching the spec)
- output/models.py (Pydantic v2 request/response models and validated types)
- output/deps.py (dependencies, including at least one yield-based dependency for cleanup and a token-based security dependency)
- output/tests/test_app.py (async tests using httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport that exercise the key endpoints)
- output/ARCHITECTURE.md (a concise explanation of the design decisions, validation approach, dependency graph, lifespan usage, error handling, and testing strategy)

Requirements you must meet:
- Use an async lifespan context manager to initialize and clean up shared resources; store shared state on app.state.
- Demonstrate a yield dependency with cleanup code that runs even on errors.
- Use Pydantic v2 patterns: Field(default_factory=...) for mutable defaults, model_dump instead of .dict, and at least one Annotated[...] with Field constraints (e.g., min_length).
- Include at least one POST endpoint that returns 201 on creation and returns a dict payload (not a Pydantic model), and at least one plain-text endpoint using Response(media_type="text/plain").
- Use proper error handling with raise HTTPException and avoid time.sleep in async code; if you need to simulate latency, use await asyncio.sleep().
- Demonstrate BackgroundTasks usage and note limitations in the docs.
- Include a simple token-based security dependency (e.g., OAuth2PasswordBearer for documentation) with your own validation logic inside the dependency.
- In tests, use httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport to call the in-process app and cover at least the POST creation path and one error path.
- Do not add external services or databases; use in-memory stubs only.

Write all outputs under output/ exactly as specified above.
